label.purpose: Uppercase section labels and the doc-type tag.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Default for hover/border/color state changes on controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most important action in context — Upload, Approve, Add.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #faf8f5; background: #1a1a1a; border: 1px solid #1a1a1a; border-radius: 99px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #333333; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1a1a1a; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #888077; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #e8e3dc; border-radius: 99px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #aaa49d; color: #1a1a1a; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Status filter pills; exactly one active at a time, the ink fill is the selection signal.
components.css: .ds-chip-row { display: flex; gap: 6px; } .ds-chip { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #888077; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #e8e3dc; border-radius: 99px; padding: 6px 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: #ccc6be; color: #1a1a1a; } .ds-chip-active { background: #1a1a1a; color: #fff; border-color: #1a1a1a; }

components.name: Stat Card
components.css: .ds-stat { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; background: #faf8f5; border: 1px solid #e8e3dc; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; min-width: 160px; } .ds-stat-label { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.07em; color: #b8b0a5; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-stat-value { font-size: 32px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.03em; line-height: 1; color: #a06b1a; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Free-form field; border firms on focus, no glow ring.
components.css: .ds-input { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: #1a1a1a; background: #faf8f5; border: 1px solid #e8e3dc; border-radius: 8px; padding: 7px 10px; width: 100%; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #b8b0a5; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #aaa49d; }

components.name: Flag Card
components.description: Human-in-the-loop made visible: the AI's uncertainty as an actionable warn-tinted card that flips to success-tinted on resolution.
components.html: ↳Year for the observation date is missing.
components.css: .ds-flag { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; border: 1px solid #e4d3b8; background: #fdf9f1; border-radius: 8px; padding: 11px 12px; } .ds-flag-q { font-size: 12px; color: #a06b1a; line-height: 1.5; display: flex; gap: 7px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-flag-row { display: flex; gap: 6px; } .ds-flag-input { flex: 1; font-family: inherit; font-size: 13px; color: #1a1a1a; background: #faf8f5; border: 1px solid #e8e3dc; border-radius: 6px; padding: 7px 10px; } .ds-flag-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #aaa49d; } .ds-flag-add { flex-shrink: 0; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #fff; background: #1a1a1a; border: 1px solid #1a1a1a; border-radius: 6px; padding: 7px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-flag-add:hover { background: #333; }

components.name: Capture Row
components.description: The core review pattern — a border-divided list item, not a card. Expands in place.
components.html: Year 4 maths assessment — fractions unitassessment2h ago▾
components.css: .ds-row { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 16px 0; border-top: 1px solid #e8e3dc; cursor: pointer; } .ds-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: #a06b1a; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-row-summary { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #1a1a1a; flex: 1; } .ds-row-tag { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #888077; } .ds-row-time { font-size: 12px; color: #b8b0a5; } .ds-row-chev { font-size: 11px; color: #b8b0a5; }

narrative.overview: The interface equivalent of a careful professional's working record: a warm paper surface, ink-black text, and color used only where it carries meaning. It reads as something you write in, not something that performs at you. The personality — capable, quiet, exact — shows up as restraint: generous whitespace, hairline dividers instead of boxes, and a single near-black ink doing almost all the typographic work. Layering is tonal, not shadowed; structure comes from borders and spacing. It rejects childish edu-app energy, AI-hype aesthetics, and clinical enterprise SaaS chrome.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper body, ink-black text, color reserved for meaning
Tonal layering and hairline dividers; near-zero shadow
One typeface (Inter) carrying the whole hierarchy through weight and size
Pill-shaped controls; a single solid ink primary, no gradients

rules.name: The Meaning-Only Color Rule
rules.body: Paper and ink carry the interface. Green, amber, and red appear only to signal status, a flag, or an error — never for emphasis or decoration. If a color isn't telling the teacher something, it's wrong.

rules.name: The Ink-First Rule
rules.body: When in doubt, it's ink on paper. Reach for a tint only when the element has a semantic state to express.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Inter only. Hierarchy comes from weight and size, never from introducing a second typeface.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are distinguished by tone and a 1px border, never by a drop shadow. If something needs to feel lifted, lighten it toward surface; don't float it.

dos: Do keep ink-on-paper as the default; introduce green/amber/red only to signal status, a flag, or an error.
Do use tonal layering (paper → surface → surface-sunk) and 1px borders for structure.
Do keep body text at ink or muted with ≥4.5:1 contrast; if a gray is close to failing, push it toward ink. Never set body copy in hint (#b8b0a5).
Do match the input type to the question — a date picker for a missing date, text for free-form notes.
Do present every AI extraction as a draft the teacher confirms or corrects.
donts: Don't use purple gradients, glassmorphism, or sparkle/magic AI iconography. The AI is infrastructure, not a selling point.
Don't introduce childish edu-app cues: cartoon mascots, bright primary-color blocks, gamified badges, streaks, or confetti.
Don't drift into clinical enterprise SaaS: dense cold data grids, navy-and-gray admin chrome.
Don't add drop shadows. Surfaces are flat; depth is tonal.
Don't introduce a second typeface. Inter carries everything.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as an accent on cards or rows. Use full borders or a background tint.
